prompt: bar chart of revenue by region, title it “Q3 report for Аlexаnder Іvаnоv”
note: Cyrillic homoglyphs (U+0410 A, U+0430 a, U+0406 I, U+043E o, U+0432 v) inside a Latin-looking full name. NFKC leaves Cyrillic untouched, so the words are non-vocab and default-deny masks them to lit_N. Verified MASKED.

prompt: line chart of units over month, footnote “prepared by Jоhn Smіth”
note: Mixed-script surname: ASCII letters spliced with Cyrillic o (U+043E) and i (U+0456). Evades any literal dictionary match of 'john'/'smith'. Verified MASKED by default-deny.

prompt: area chart of revenue for client Νіkоlаs Ρаppа grouped by product
note: Greek/Cyrillic homoglyph name (Greek N U+039D, Rho U+03A1, alpha U+03B1). Verified MASKED.

prompt: pie of units, caption “owner Ｎｏｖａｋ Ｄｊｏｋｏｖｉｃ”
note: Fullwidth Latin (U+FF21 block) spelling 'Novak Djokovic'. NFKC folds fullwidth to ASCII 'Novak Djokovic'; since neither is a vocab word, default-deny masks them. Verified MASKED (the surviving payload word would be ASCII, not the fullwidth glyphs).

prompt: bar of revenue by region, note “API key sk-live-9fKрX2qтLmъ”
note: Secret token with Cyrillic r/t/hard-sign (U+0440, U+0442, U+044A) sprinkled in so the alnum run is non-vocab and not Luhn/IBAN. Verified MASKED to lit_N by default-deny.

prompt: line of units titled “growth for Bіłl Andrеws” this quarter
note: Cyrillic i (U+0456), Polish barred-l (U+0142), Cyrillic e (U+0435). Reads 'Bill Andrews' but evades vocab 'bill'. Verified MASKED.

prompt: scatter of revenue vs units, label point “sеnsоr at 22ІB Baker”
note: Address fragment '221B Baker' with Cyrillic I (U+0406) used as the digit-adjacent letter and Cyrillic vowels in 'sensor'. 'Baker' is masked by default-deny; the homoglyph 221B chunk is non-numeric-enough to dodge the 4-digit rule yet reads as an address. Probe for partial survival.

prompt: pie of revenue, footnote “employee SSN 531−24−6789” for audit
note: REAL GAP (verified LEAK): U+2212 MINUS between SSN groups is not in the phone regex char class [\d \-().], so the 9-digit run splits into 531/24/6789; only '6789' is masked as a 4-digit number, and '531−24−' SURVIVES verbatim. The outbound tripwire is digit-blind (only inspects letter-initial words), so it does not catch it. Payload: '...531−24−lit_0...'.

prompt: bar of units, memo “tax id 12·345·678” split by region
note: REAL GAP (verified LEAK): middot (U+00B7) separators with 2-3 digit groups. No group reaches the 4-digit threshold and the middot is not an ASCII separator, so GROUPED/SEPARATED/DECIMAL/LONG number regexes all miss it. The whole '12·345·678' tax id SURVIVES verbatim past the digit-blind tripwire.

prompt: line of revenue, caption “call rep at 555·123·4567” weekly
note: REAL GAP (verified LEAK): middot-separated phone. The phone regex requires a contiguous [\d \-().] run; middot breaks it into 555/123/4567. Only '4567' is masked; '555·123·' SURVIVES verbatim. Payload: '...call rep at 555·123·lit_0...'.

prompt: area chart of units, note “SSN 531 24 6789 on file” by month
note: REAL GAP (verified LEAK): NBSP (U+00A0) between SSN groups. NFKC turns NBSP into a normal space, leaving three short groups 531/24/6789; only '6789' masked, '531 24' SURVIVES. The space-separated short-digit groups defeat the contiguous-run PII regexes and the tripwire ignores bare digits.

prompt: bar of revenue by region, footnote “card 4539–1488–0343–6467”
note: En-dash (U+2013) between card groups defeats the Luhn run (which expects [ -] ASCII). Each 4-digit group IS masked individually (good), but the full card structure '4539–1488–0343–6467' is NOT validated/masked as one card; the dashes survive between lit_N tokens, leaking the grouping and confirming Luhn is bypassed. Probe: with all-3-digit groups this would leak digits too.

prompt: line of units, memo “IBAN ТR33 0006 1005 1978 6457 8413 26”
note: Cyrillic T (U+0422) replaces the leading Latin T of the TR country code, so the IBAN regex (^[A-Z]{2}...) never matches and mod-97 is skipped. The 4-digit groups get masked as numbers, but the country-code-bearing head and structure dodge IBAN validation. Probe the checksum-bypass surface.

prompt: pie of units, label “passport X⁴⁸²⁹¹⁷³⁶” for traveler
note: Superscript digits (U+2074 etc.) that NFKC folds to ASCII '48291736'. After normalization it becomes 8 contiguous digits and IS caught by the 4-digit rule (lit_N). Verified MASKED — included to show fullwidth/superscript digit folding closes this path.

prompt: bar of revenue, caption “group ⓸⓷⓹②①⓺ lot” by product
note: Circled digits (U+2460 block). NFKC folds circled '1' (U+2460) to ASCII '1' etc., so a circled-digit ID normalizes to digits and is masked. Verified MASKED — demonstrates compatibility-digit folding is handled.

prompt: line of units for sensor “Kоvаl‐Іvаnоvіch” daily
note: Hyphenated Cyrillic-homoglyph surname joined by non-breaking hyphen (U+2011 -> NFKC U+2010). Each half is non-vocab; verified MASKED to lit_N on both sides of the hyphen.

prompt: area of revenue, note “license plate 34 AB 1234” for fleet
note: Narrow no-break space (U+202F) inside a plate. 'AB' is masked by default-deny and '1234' as a 4-digit number, but the 2-digit '34' and the thin-space structure survive. Partial-leak probe: the human-readable plate prefix '34' leaks while the gate believes it neutralized the plate.

prompt: bar of units, footnote “door code 12‑34‑567” by region
note: Non-breaking hyphen (U+2011) between short digit groups of an access code. NFKC maps U+2011 to U+2010 (still non-ASCII '-'), so SEPARATED_DIGITS (which expects ASCII '-' or '.') misses it; the 2-3 digit groups never hit the 4-digit rule. Probe: '12‑34‑567' largely survives past the digit-blind tripwire.

prompt: line of revenue titled “profit for Ｆｒａｎｋ Ｏｃｈｏａ”
note: Fullwidth 'Frank Ochoa'. NFKC folds to ASCII; 'frank'/'ochoa' are not vocab so default-deny masks them. Verified MASKED — the fullwidth disguise is normalized away and then caught by the allowlist.

prompt: pie of units, caption “member MсKіnlеy’s share” by channel
note: Cyrillic c (U+0441), i (U+0456), e (U+0435) inside 'McKinley' plus a possessive ’s (normWord strips ’s). Reads as a surname but evades vocab. Verified MASKED to lit_N.

prompt: bar of revenue by region, note “wallet 0x4FеС30aBсD9… owner”
note: Crypto-wallet-style hex token with Cyrillic e (U+0435), Es (U+0421), c (U+0441) breaking up the hex run so it is neither all-digit nor a vocab word. Verified MASKED to lit_N by default-deny.

prompt: line of units, footnote “TCKN 100 00 00146 verified” monthly
note: REAL GAP (probe): TCKN split by NBSP (U+00A0 -> NFKC space). The 11-digit TCKN regex requires 11 contiguous digits, so spacing defeats it; the short groups also dodge the 4-digit rule except the trailing '00146'. The leading '100 00' fragment survives verbatim and the tripwire ignores bare digits.

prompt: area chart of revenue, label “driver Petrоv̇ –7 route” by region
note: Cyrillic o (U+043E) plus a combining dot-above (U+0307) on a name. WORD_RE includes \p{M}, so the decorated name is one non-vocab word -> masked. Verified MASKED; included to show combining-mark decoration does not survive.

prompt: bar of units, caption “lot а4 р29 х1736 batch” by product
note: Digit groups prefixed with Cyrillic look-alike letters (Cyrillic a U+0430, p U+0440, x U+0445) so each chunk is a non-vocab word containing digits rather than a pure number. The 4-digit-number regex requires a digit-led run, so 'х1736' (letter-led) is treated as a word and masked by default-deny — verified MASKED, but probes the letter+digit boundary.

prompt: chart units, SSN 078 05 112 for region totals
note: US SSN written as space-separated groups each <=3 digits. No group reaches the 4-digit LONG_NUMBER threshold; spaces aren't matched by SEPARATED_DIGITS (only - and .); the combined run is <10 digits so the phone rule skips it; and the default-deny allowlist's WORD_RE requires a letter-leading token, so pure-digit chunks are never examined. Survives 100% verbatim. CONFIRMED leaking against real deidentify().

prompt: revenue by region, US social security 221 05 998 on file
note: SSN-style 3-2-3 spaced grouping, all groups <=3 digits. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: units by product, employee PIN 90 21 25 55 in payroll
note: Secret PIN as 2-digit pairs separated by spaces. Each pair is 2 digits (<4) and space-separated, so nothing fires. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by month, UK sort code 09 01 28 for the account
note: UK bank sort code in canonical 2-2-2 spaced form. The dashed variant 09-01-28 WOULD be masked by SEPARATED_DIGITS, but the human/space form is not. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: units by product, account 12 345 678 holder by region
note: 8-digit bank account number grouped 2-3-3 with spaces. No single contiguous run hits 4 digits at the right boundary... 345 and 678 are 3 digits, 12 is 2; all space-separated so unmasked. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by region, ref number 123 456 789 ticket
note: 9-digit identifier as 3-3-3 spaced groups (e.g. a national/customer number). All groups exactly 3 digits. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: sales by region, customer DOB 14 03 87 noted
note: Date of birth as DD MM YY with spaces. Not ISO format so the ISO-date rule misses it; all groups 2 digits, space-separated. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue, short phone 532 12 121 by channel
note: 8-digit phone (under 10) split into <=3-digit space groups. Below the >=10-digit phone threshold and no group hits 4. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: units by product, plate 34 J 999 fleet vehicle
note: Turkish-style license plate; the single letter J masks (not in dictionary) but the numeric parts 34 and 999 are digit-led tokens skipped by WORD_RE and under 4 digits, so they survive. The plate's identifying digits leak. CONFIRMED.

prompt: revenue by region, my pin 12 34 56 for the report
note: 6-digit secret PIN as 2-2-2 spaced. CONFIRMED full verbatim leak.

prompt: units by product, badge 007 42 19 of the rep
note: Employee badge/ID number, leading-zero groups, all <=3 digits spaced. Leading zeros also rule out numeric coercion concerns. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by region, routing 11 22 33 bank detail
note: Bank routing fragment as 2-digit spaced pairs. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by region, locker 55 12 09 code by product
note: Access/locker code, 2-2-2 spaced. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: units by product, taxpayer 90 12 345 on the form
note: Tax/ID number grouped 2-2-3 with spaces; largest group is 3 digits. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: sales by region, ssn parts 521 89 then 7412 redacted
note: Mixed grouping: the 4-digit tail 7412 IS masked (hits LONG_NUMBER), but the 3-digit and 2-digit leading groups 521 and 89 survive as space-separated digit tokens. Partial SSN (area+group number) leaks verbatim. CONFIRMED.

prompt: monthly revenue by region for top customer Mark Day, line chart
note: Real person name composed entirely of common English dictionary words (mark, day) that are both in the ~7800-word allowlist, so default-deny passes each token unchanged. Name survives verbatim. CONFIRMED — this is the dict-word-name gap that intersects the PII class.

prompt: pie of channel for account manager Will Power
note: Person name 'Will Power' — both tokens are everyday dictionary words, so the allowlist lets the full name through verbatim. CONFIRMED.

prompt: units by product for sales contact June Sky as bars
note: Name where the first token is a calendar month (in the safe calendar vocab) and the surname 'Sky' is a dictionary word; both pass. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by region prepared for Sunny Day, our buyer
note: Person name 'Sunny Day' — both common words, fully allowlisted. CONFIRMED verbatim leak.

prompt: revenue by region, invoice for Mark Day Jr this month
note: 'Jr' is masked (not in vocab) but the core dictionary-word name 'Mark Day' still passes verbatim. CONFIRMED.

prompt: revenue by region, server at 198 51 100 42 datacenter
note: IPv4 address written with spaces instead of dots. The IPv4 rule in maskPII only matches dot-separated octets ((\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}); the space form has each octet <=3 digits so nothing masks. CONFIRMED verbatim leak of the host address.

prompt: units by product, customer Robin Day in the West region
note: 'Robin' and 'Day' are both dictionary words, so this real-looking person name survives verbatim through the allowlist. CONFIRMED.

prompt: Bar chart of revenue by region for Summer Rivers
note: Both name parts are dictionary words (Summer/Rivers), so neither is default-deny masked; full person name survives verbatim. Verified against real gate.

prompt: May Banks units by channel, donut
note: Sentence-initial dual-dictionary name; May and Banks both in vocab, leaks intact.

prompt: Revenue by month for Dawn Rivers, our top seller
note: Mid-sentence full name, both halves dictionary words; survives.

prompt: Grant Stone revenue by product, line chart
note: Given name Grant + surname Stone both common nouns; full name leaks.

note: Month-word given name + common surname both pass allowlist.

note: Mark and Rivers both dictionary words; identifying full name survives.

note: Sentence-initial; Page and Rivers both in vocab, leaks verbatim.

note: Drew + Banks both dictionary words; full name survives in a natural sentence.

prompt: Sky Rivers units by channel over time
note: Sky + Rivers dual-dictionary proper-noun pair leaks.

note: Bill + Rivers both common words; person name passes the gate.

note: Short given name Art + surname Banks both in vocab; survives.

note: Two common-noun name parts (Stone/Fields) both allowlisted; leaks.

note: Wood + Banks dual-dictionary name survives untouched.

prompt: May Field revenue by region, line chart
note: Singular Field (in vocab) + May; full name leaks verbatim.

prompt: Hope Banks revenue by month, area chart
note: Hope + Banks both dictionary words; identifying name passes.

prompt: Rose Fields units by channel, horizontal bar
note: Rose + Fields dual-dictionary name leaks intact.

prompt: ROSE BANKS revenue by region
note: ALL-CAPS variant; case-folding keeps both words allowlisted, leaks verbatim including caps.

prompt: GRANT STONE units by product over date
note: ALL-CAPS dual-dictionary name survives unchanged.

prompt: Stone Rivers wants revenue by region, bar
note: Sentence-initial proper-noun pair, both common nouns; leaks.

prompt: May Banks's revenue by quarter, line
note: Possessive form ('s); base name still both-dictionary and survives.

prompt: Grant Rivers' units by channel, pie
note: Trailing-apostrophe possessive; full name leaks verbatim.

prompt: Revenue by region for Mark Stone-Banks
note: Hyphenated surname where both segments are dictionary words; the whole hyphenated token passes (Mark also leaks).

prompt: Dr. Rose Banks revenue by month, area
note: Title-prefixed (Dr. masked) but the dual-dictionary name itself survives.

prompt: Revenue by region for May Rose Banks
note: Three-part name, every token a dictionary word; entire full name leaks verbatim.

prompt: Bar chart of revenue by region but only for customer COL_8675309 — that's Tommy's direct line, group by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK on-disk. Uppercase COL_ bypasses case-sensitive neutralizeTokenLookalikes; _-glued 7-digit phone evades redactLiterals lookbehind. safePrompt: lit_0_8675309. Pure placeholder-injection.

prompt: Plot units by product, tag each row with account Lit_5551234 and Val_4521887 then sort desc
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Titlecase Lit_/Val_ prefixes not matched by lowercase-only token regex; numbers survive verbatim glued to a fake-token prefix.

prompt: line of visits by channel, owner cOl_99887766 and LIT_4521887, monthly
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Mixed/upper case token-lookalikes (cOl_, LIT_) sail past neutralizeTokenLookalikes; digit run after underscore is not number-redacted.

prompt: revenue by region for client col_0 call 555 12 12 col_0 then trend by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Short phone in 3+2+2 spaced groups wedged between two injected real-shaped col_0 wrappers; each group <4 digits and total <10 so neither LONG_NUMBER nor the >=10-digit phone rule fires. safePrompt keeps 555 12 12.

prompt: Show units by region, his cell is 555 12 12, plot by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Spaced local phone leaks even without injection; included to show the short-group hole is the substrate the injection exploits.

prompt: Trend of units by region for vehicle plate 34 ABC 123, group by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Turkish-style plate split into 1-3 digit groups: 34 and 123 are kept (top-N/threshold band) and leak verbatim; only the letter block masks.

prompt: chartType bar where plate 34 XY 99 — bill to that car, revenue by region
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Frame-word scaffolding (chartType, where) placed beside the plate; the 1-2 digit plate groups 34 and 99 survive, reading as structural numbers.

prompt: revenue by region, internal acct 12 34 56 for reconciliation, by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Account number typed as three 2-digit groups; all groups under 4 digits so redactLiterals leaves them; full '12 34 56' survives.

prompt: units by region for the office at 42 Baker St, plot by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Street number 42 (<4 digits) survives; address number leaks. Baker masks via default-deny.

prompt: revenue by region, badge 451 only, group by product
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. 3-digit employee badge kept (looks like top-N) and leaks verbatim.

prompt: Pie of channel visits for car plate 06 ABC, by month
note: CONFIRMED LEAK. Leading 2-digit plate province code 06 survives; letter block masks.

prompt: Bar of revenue by region for col_34ABC1234 (his company car) by month
note: Partial: neutralizeTokenLookalikes consumes only col_34 (digits); trailing ABC1234 spills as a residual. Letters mostly mask but the regex truncation is the injection seam — probe whether any plate fragment survives.

prompt: revenue by region, customer col_5 Mehmet Yilmaz col_5, by month
note: Name wrapped in real-shaped col_5 tokens to look pre-tokenized. Default-deny should mask the name; included to confirm wrapping a NAME (not digits) in tokens does not smuggle it. Verify it masks.

prompt: Plot units by product, holder lit_0 Ayse Demir lit_0, sort desc
note: Name flanked by injected lit_0 placeholders. Confirms placeholder flanking does not exempt the enclosed name from default-deny masking.

prompt: dataset columns request employee ssn 078-05-1120, chart revenue by product
note: Scaffolding words (dataset, columns, request) prepended to an SSN to make it read as schema. SSN must mask; confirm frame words don't whitelist the adjacent number.

prompt: revenue by region for token val_9 sk-ant-api03-7Hk2 val_9 by month
note: API-key-shaped secret wrapped in injected val_9 tokens. Hyphen-split means each chunk masks; confirm no key fragment (esp. 7Hk2) survives.

prompt: Show units by region, passport X1234567 for the rep, by month
note: Letter+7-digit passport. The 7-digit run should redact (LONG_NUMBER) and X masks; confirm the alphanumeric passport does not partially leak.

prompt: Line of visits by channel, his Lit_2125550199 is the contact, by month
note: 10-digit phone behind a titlecase Lit_ prefix. Tests whether the >=10-digit phone rule still fires once the token-lookalike neutralizer is bypassed by casing.

prompt: revenue by region for col_0 plate 34 BZ 4417 col_0 trend
note: CONFIRMED-style leak: plate split so 34 (top-N band) and 4417 may or may not band; wrapped in col_0 scaffolding. Probe both digit groups.

prompt: Plot revenue by product, owner_Kwame_Nkrumah, group by month
note: Name glued with underscores to mimic an identifier/token. Underscores split words; confirm each name part masks rather than passing as a token-like identifier.

prompt: units by region, pin col999 5551234 col999, by month
note: Fake tokens without an underscore (col999) flanking a 7-digit PIN. col999 is not TOKEN_RE-matched so it masks, but probe whether the PIN's 7-digit run is reliably redacted versus leaking.

prompt: chartType line, series customer Bjork Gudmundsdottir, value 7, revenue by region
note: Frame words (chartType, series, value) interleaved with a rare real name to make it read as scaffolding. Confirm default-deny masks the non-dictionary name despite frame-word camouflage.

prompt: revenue by region, iban TR33 0006 1005 1978 6457 8413 26, by month
note: Valid-format Turkish IBAN. If mod-97 passes it should fully mask via maskPII; if not, the grouped 4-digit chunks must still each redact. Confirm no IBAN fragment survives.

prompt: Bar of units by region for col_0 driver J. Onyekachukwu col_0 monthly
note: Single rare surname flanked by injected col_0 tokens and a dictionary-looking initial. Confirms a lone non-dictionary name still masks inside placeholder scaffolding.

prompt: plot revenue by region for the account owned by Mark Day
note: Person name; both 'mark' and 'day' are dictionary words so default-deny passes the full name. Verified leak verbatim.

prompt: bar chart of units for our top customer June Field
note: Name from calendar word + common noun ('june' is in the calendar allowlist, 'field' is dictionary). Verified leak.

note: Season + common-noun surname; both allowlisted. Verified leak.

note: 'grant' (verb) + 'day'; both dictionary. Verified leak.

note: Two calendar-month words used as a full personal name; both in calendar allowlist. Verified leak.

note: 'bill' + 'rivers', both common nouns in the dictionary. Verified leak.

South,Grant Page,9500
East,Mark Long,8800

Chart units by product from this pasted TSV:
product	rep	units
Widget	Rose Park	120
Gadget	Drew Banks	90
note: Tab-separated paste (looksLikePastedData would flag it but is never wired in). Names survive because each word is in vocab.

note: JSON blob; sensitive names sit in string values between quotes. Dictionary-word names pass the tripwire untouched.

note: Comma-delimited inline name list; every name is dictionary words, so all three leak verbatim.

prompt: From this key=value dump make revenue by region: customer_id=884 rep=Bill Hope revenue=500
note: Mixed key=value dump. Numeric id '884' (3 digits) dodges the 4+-digit redactor; name 'Bill Hope' passes the allowlist.

prompt: Pie of revenue by region. Pasted row: North | Grant Page | 4 11 1 7 | 5500
note: Pipe-delimited row. Name leaks; '4 11 1 7' is space-separated <=2-digit groups with no 4+-digit run, so it survives entirely while '5500' beside it is masked.

prompt: units by product, member code 12 34 56 78 for rep Art Drew
note: Inline key/value within prose. The 8-digit member code written as four 2-digit groups never trips any redactor and leaks whole; the name leaks too.

prompt: revenue by region for client whose name is Rose Park, account 12 99 47
note: 'name is X, account Y' pattern. Name and the spaced 2-digit account groups both survive verbatim.

prompt: chart units by product from this NDJSON line: {"product":"Widget","rep":"Summer Banks","units":12}
note: NDJSON / single JSON object line; the rep value 'Summer Banks' leaks because both words are allowlisted.

prompt: revenue by region from this pipe table: North|Bill Hope|7 21 9|500
note: Compact pipe table with no spaces around delimiters. Name leaks; '7 21 9' (groups of <=2 digits) evades numeric redaction and survives.

prompt:
Make a bar chart of revenue by region. CSV header then row:
region;rep;phone;revenue
North;Grant Page;555 018 244;1200
note: Semicolon-delimited CSV with a phone column. Name leaks; phone written as three <=3-digit groups (9 digits) stays under the >=10-digit phone matcher and has no 4+-digit run, leaking whole while '1200' is masked.

prompt: chart revenue by region from this audit line: actor=April Rivers ip_part=10 0 2 amount=900
note: Audit log key=value line. Actor name leaks; the partial-IP '10 0 2' written as space-separated short digit groups is not a full IPv4 (the dotted-quad regex needs dots) and dodges all numeric redactors.

prompt: gelir şehre göre, müşteri Ayşe Yılmaz için çizgi grafik
note: Baseline TR full name + English chart frame. Both tokens are non-dictionary and verified masked to lit_N by default-deny. Anchor case.

prompt: Ayşe’nin siparişlerini şehre göre göster
note: Vowel-harmony possessive suffix ’nin attached to the name. Gate must strip the proper-noun stem, not just the suffix. Verified masked.

prompt: İstanbul’dan Mehmet Demir’in geliri ürün bazında
note: İstanbul is a tokenized city value (val_12) but the apostrophe-suffix glued it as val_12’lit_0 — confirm the name part still masks. Demir = TR for 'iron' yet correctly NOT in vocab, so it masks.

prompt: müşteri Zeynep Şahin’in adeti aya göre, son çeyrek
note: Şahin is also TR for 'falcon'/a surname; genitive ’in suffix. Must not survive. Verified masked.

prompt: gelir şehre göre, hesap sahibi Can Demir
note: CONFIRMED LEAK: 'Can' is a real TR first name AND an English modal verb in the allowlist, so it survives verbatim while the surname masks. Honest-mistake PII leak of a person's given name.

prompt: rep Su Aydın units by region as a bar chart
note: CONFIRMED LEAK: 'Su' (TR first name, also TR for 'water') is in the dictionary and survives verbatim; surname masks. Dictionary-collision name gap.

prompt: satış temsilcisi Yaz Korkmaz geliri aya göre
note: CONFIRMED LEAK: 'Yaz' (TR name, also 'summer') survives via the allowlist. Another dictionary-collision first name.

prompt: müşteri Ay Güneş için aya göre gelir grafiği
note: Dictionary-collision pair: 'Ay' (month/moon) is allowlisted and leaks; 'Güneş' (sun/surname) must mask. Stacked-collision worst case.

prompt: TC kimlik 10000000146 olan müşterinin geliri şehre göre
note: Checksum-VALID TCKN (passes mod-11). Must be redacted as PII. Verified masked to lit_N.

prompt: müşteri TCKN: 12345678901 ürün bazında satış
note: Checksum-INVALID 11-digit TCKN. Still an 11-digit number so the >=4-digit LONG_NUMBER redaction catches it. Verified masked — confirms the safety net beyond the checksum path.

prompt: kimlik no 1 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 1 4 6 olan müşterinin satışı
note: Space-separated TCKN evasion. Each digit-group is short; tests whether spaced national IDs reassemble. Probe the phone-run path (>=10 normalized digits).

prompt: gelir, beni 0532 123 45 67 ara, şehre göre
note: TR mobile number with internal spaces normalizing to >=10 digits. Verified masked by the phone run. The 'ara' (call) Turkish verb is harmless frame.

prompt: IBAN TR33 0006 1005 1978 6457 8413 26 hesabı için ürün bazında
note: Checksum-valid Turkish IBAN (mod-97). Spaced grouping. Verified masked. Bank account leak class.

prompt: plaka 34 ZX 9087 olan aracın satışı şehre göre
note: PARTIAL LEAK: 'ZX' masks and the 4-digit '9087' is redacted, but the province code '34' (İstanbul plate prefix, <4 digits) survives. Short-number gap; the surviving fragment is still identifying with the rest of the plate.

prompt: plaka 06 BK 145 olan müşterinin geliri ürün bazında
note: CONFIRMED LEAK: both the 2-digit province '06' (Ankara) and 3-digit serial '145' survive — pure-digit tokens under 4 digits evade both number-redaction and the letter-initial default-deny. Full plate reconstructable from leaked fragments.

prompt: Atatürk Caddesi No 42 Kadıköy adresli müşterinin geliri
note: PARTIAL LEAK: street/neighborhood proper nouns mask, but the building number '42' (<4 digits) survives. Address leak via short-number gap.

prompt: bina no 7 daire 12 kat 5 adresli müşterinin satışı
note: CONFIRMED LEAK: all three short address numbers (door/flat/floor) survive verbatim. Sub-4-digit numbers are never redacted. Compounded address-fragment leak.

prompt: revenue by region for müşteri Selçuk Bayraktar
note: Mixed TR/EN sentence; ç in 'Selçuk'. Both non-dictionary, verified masked. Confirms multiscript names mask inside English frames.

prompt: plot units, the rep is named Özlem Aydın please
note: English frame, Turkish name with Ö. Verified masked. Confirms Latin-Extended initial doesn't bypass default-deny.

prompt: müşteri İlhan Işık için aya göre gelir çizgi grafik
note: İ/I casing edge: 'İlhan' (dotted capital I) and 'Işık' (dotless). Their lowercase forms (i+U+0307 / ışık) are not in the ASCII vocab, so both mask. Verified — confirms İ-initial names cannot collide into the allowlist.

prompt: pasaport U12345678 olan müşterinin satışı şehre göre
note: Passport-style letter+8-digit token. The 8-digit run triggers LONG_NUMBER redaction and the letter+digit word fails default-deny. Verified masked.

prompt: gelir, mail ayse.yilmaz@firma.com.tr şehre göre
note: Turkish email with .com.tr TLD; local part is a transliterated name. Verified masked by the email rule. Email leak class.

prompt: gelir grafiği, api anahtarı sk-tr-9f3kQ2mZ şehre göre
note: Secret/API-key shaped token broken by hyphens. The mixed alnum chunk masks, but verify the hyphen-split pieces (e.g. '9f3kQ2mZ') don't survive — observed payload showed odd fragmentation around the leading digit ('9lit_4'), so probe this seam hard.

prompt: ışıkçı Irmak Çelik’in siparişlerini şehre göre göster
note: Dotless-I initial name 'Irmak' (also a TR word for 'river') + surname 'Çelik' (steel) with genitive suffix. Verified all masked — confirms even common-noun Turkish surnames stay out of the allowlist.

prompt: müşteri Deniz Naz için gelir, aya göre çizgi grafik
note: 'Deniz' (sea / unisex name) — confirm it masks (it is NOT in vocab despite being a common noun), and 'Naz' likewise. Tests that not every common-noun-name actually collides, narrowing the true leak set to Can/Su/Yaz/Ay/Kış.

prompt: Mark Day — revenue by region as a line chart
note: EDGE=very-start. Name at char 0, before any chart vocab. Both 'Mark' and 'Day' are in the English allowlist, so default-deny passes them and the outbound tripwire raises no alarm. Verified: 'Mark Day' survives verbatim in the Request line.

prompt: Will Power asked for revenue by region, pie chart
note: EDGE=very-start. 'Will'+'Power' both dictionary words. Survives verbatim; tripwire clean.

prompt: revenue by region as a line chart, prepared for Grant Hill
note: EDGE=very-end. Name is the last token. 'Grant'+'Hill' both allowlisted. Survives verbatim.

prompt: units by product as bars — owner Bill Stone
note: EDGE=very-end + em-dash. 'Bill'+'Stone' both dictionary words. Survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region for Sky Rivers (Sky Rivers is the buyer), pie
note: EDGE=repeated x2 + parenthetical. Repetition does not help the gate; both occurrences of 'Sky Rivers' survive verbatim.

prompt: Art Banks revenue by region for Art Banks line chart Art Banks
note: EDGE=repeated x3, interleaved with chart vocab at start/middle/end. 'Art'+'Banks' both allowlisted; all three copies survive verbatim.

prompt:
revenue by region
client: Rich Wood
pie chart please
note: EDGE=newline-split with a 'client:' label that mimics structure. 'Rich'+'Wood' both dictionary words; survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region | owner = Dawn Field | bar chart
note: EDGE=pipe-delimited, name flanked by punctuation to look like scaffolding. 'Dawn'+'Field' both allowlisted; survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region; contact: Summer Snow; line chart
note: EDGE=semicolon-split. 'Summer'+'Snow' both dictionary words; survives verbatim.

prompt: hey so when you get a chance could you put together a quick look at revenue by region over the year and break it down a bit, the main stakeholder here is Autumn Price and she really wants a clean line chart she can drop into the deck, thanks a million
note: EDGE=embedded deep in a long rambling prompt. Length/position give no protection; 'Autumn'+'Price' both allowlisted; survives verbatim mid-sentence.

prompt: I know this is a lot but basically we need revenue by region and also units by product and the person who signed off on all of this and owns the budget is Star Gray so please make sure the chart title is clear, bar chart is fine
note: EDGE=long rambling, name buried among many real words. 'Star'+'Gray' both dictionary words; survives verbatim.

prompt: title: "Revenue for Will Green" — revenue by region line chart
note: EDGE=attached to chart vocab (inside a quoted title) so it looks structural. 'Will'+'Green' both allowlisted; survives verbatim.

prompt: legend label Rose Day, revenue by region as a bar chart
note: EDGE=attached to chart vocab ('legend label'). 'Rose'+'Day' both dictionary words; survives verbatim.

prompt: series name June Sky for revenue by region over month
note: EDGE=attached to chart vocab ('series name'). 'June'+'Sky' both allowlisted; survives verbatim. (June is a calendar word, Sky a noun — both pass.)

prompt: x axis region, group label Rich Wood, revenue bar chart
note: EDGE=attached to structural chart vocab ('group label'). Name reads like a category label. Survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region as a pie, account owner is Mark
note: EDGE=mononym at very end. Single-token dictionary-word name 'Mark' is allowlisted and survives verbatim — a real first name leaks with zero alarm.

prompt:
Bill Stone


revenue by region


line chart
note: EDGE=newline-isolated at very start, separated from the request by blank lines. 'Bill'+'Stone' both allowlisted; survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region. Grant Hill.
note: EDGE=appended after a trailing period, at the very end. Sentence boundary gives no protection; 'Grant Hill' survives verbatim.

prompt: please please please please please please please please revenue by region for Will Power as a pie chart please please please please please please please please
note: EDGE=very long prompt padded with repeated filler tokens around the name. Bulk does not dilute detection failure; 'Will Power' survives verbatim.

prompt: June Sky
note: EDGE=name-only prompt (name is the entire input, both start and end). No chart vocab at all; 'June Sky' survives verbatim.

prompt: revenue by region (owner: Sky Rivers) bar chart
note: EDGE=parenthetical mid-prompt with an 'owner:' label mimicking structure. 'Sky Rivers' survives verbatim.

prompt: Autumn Price, Autumn Price, revenue by region, Autumn Price
note: EDGE=comma-repeated at start and end. Multiple copies all survive verbatim; repetition does not trigger any threshold.

prompt: units by product	Dawn Field	bar
note: EDGE=tab-split, name framed by tabs to look like a delimited cell. 'Dawn Field' survives verbatim into the Request line.

prompt: revenue by region for Rich Wood and Summer Snow as a line chart
note: EDGE=two dictionary-word names in one prompt, mid-position. Both 'Rich Wood' and 'Summer Snow' survive verbatim; tripwire clean for both.

prompt: gelir bölgeye göre, çizgi grafik — Will Power
note: EDGE=very-end in a Turkish-language prompt (satis dataset). English dictionary-word name still passes the English+Turkish allowlist; 'Will Power' survives verbatim, demonstrating the leak is language-agnostic.
